data.content_ref: inline:The account claims the collapse was triggered by a synchronous systems collapse of palace redistributive economies due to a common vulnerability to drought. However, the scope explicitly excludes Egypt, which was also a highly centralized, grain-redistributing palatial economy dependent on Nile flood levels and demonstrably vulnerable to drought. The account provides no mechanism explaining why Egypt's similarly brittle administrative structure survived the same multi-year drought that allegedly caused the synchronous collapse of all other palace systems. This differential outcome undermines the claim that a common structural vulnerability to drought was the primary cause.
